Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 24-bit audio byte packing for paInt24 format

paInt24 expects exactly 3 bytes per sample, but np.int32.tobytes()
produces 4 bytes — causing byte boundary misalignment and loud noise.

Fix: convert int32 buffer to little-endian uint8 view, reshape to
(-1, 4) and slice [:, :3] to keep only the 3 significant bytes per
sample before sending to PyAudio.

Silence paths updated to use bytes(N*2*3) which correctly produces
3 bytes per sample × 2 channels without any dtype conversion.

commit.message:
v0.4.0: Dedicated parsers for translation formats (SDLXLIFF, XLIFF, TMX, PO)

SDLXLIFF/XLIFF files now use dedicated XML parsers that extract only
translatable source (or target) segments, instead of dumping all raw
content via Tika. Inline tags are properly handled. Source/target
toggle added to the UI with auto-detected language codes.

Fixes wildly inflated word counts for SDLXLIFF files (e.g. 26k → 7k).

commit.message:
Bump version to 0.4.2: Fix all REPORT2.txt issues

BUG-1: intercept_wp_notification_email now only suppresses WP's email
  for self-registered users; admin-created users still receive their
  standard credential/set-password email.

BUG-2: Remove orphaned msc_licence field — removed from $meta_fields
  in ajax_update_profile() and from FormData in frontend.js. Previously
  the non-existent #pe_licence input caused an empty string to be saved
  on every profile update, silently clearing stored data.

BUG-3: Fix set_font_size() called with 3 args in class-indemnity.php
  (FOR MINORS heading, physical-signature path). The third arg was
  silently dropped by PHP.

SEC-2: Add server-side 5MB file size check in ajax_upload_profile_photo()
  before relying solely on the JS-side enforcement.

QA-1: Replace deprecated current_time('timestamp') with time() in
  class-shortcodes.php (was fixed in class-registration.php previously
  but missed here).

QA-2: Replace current_time('mysql') with gmdate('Y-m-d H:i:s') in
  class-registration.php to store UTC dates consistently.

QA-3: Clarify set_font_size magic number comment in class-indemnity.php.

QA-4: Rename unused $blogname param to $_blogname in intercept method.

commit.message:
Fix Book 7 pivoted CTE: pre-compute DATE_SUB cutoffs in unpvt CTE

Move DATE_SUB(END_DTTM, 365/730) out of nested COUNT(CASE WHEN...)
expressions into the unpvt CTE as cutoff_12mo/cutoff_24mo columns.
DBR 15.4 ML can't resolve column names through deeply nested
function calls inside aggregate expressions.

commit.message:
Fix three math and logic bugs in titrations calculator

- calculate_initial_pH: was using the approximation sqrt(C*Ka) which
  assumes x is negligible. Now solves the exact quadratic
  x^2 + Ka*x - Ka*C = 0, matching the approach of the original code.

- weak_acid_pH_calculator after-equivalence branch: was calling
  Henderson-Hasselbalch with (Ca*Va - Cb*Vb) in the denominator, which
  goes negative past the equivalence point causing log(negative) to
  crash. Replaced with the correct formula: [OH-] = excess_moles /
  total_volume, pOH = -log([OH-]), pH = 14 - pOH.

- weak_acid_pH_calculator equivalence branch: was wrapping
  calculate_equivalence_point() in print(), which printed "None" after
  that function's own output since it returns None. Removed outer print.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix two bugs in dissociation calculator

- determine_monoprotic_acid_dissociation_equation: was calling print()
  internally for the H==None, A==None (full dissociation) case and
  returning None, causing the caller to print "None". Changed to return
  the result string so the caller's print() handles output correctly.

- polyprotic_dissociations: positive_roots was set with max(generator),
  which returns a single float. Calling max() on that float then threw
  TypeError: float object is not iterable. Fixed to use a list
  comprehension so max() receives a proper list.

failures in FocusMode where the callback was never wired. Also adds
production access docs to CLAUDE.md.

commit.message:
fix(hermeneus): OAuth tokens need Bearer header + beta flag (#518)

## Summary

The Anthropic API supports OAuth behind `anthropic-beta:
oauth-2025-04-20`. The TS runtime used the SDK's `authToken` param which
sets both `Authorization: Bearer` and the beta header. The Rust client
was missing both.

Branch on `CredentialSource::OAuth` in `build_headers()` to send Bearer
+ beta flag. API key path unchanged.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace` — zero warnings
- [x] All hermeneus tests pass
- [ ] Deploy to worker-node, run eval — conversation tests should pass

Co-authored-by: admin <admin@ardentleatherworks.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade audio output to 24-bit format

- Changed PyAudio format from paInt16 to paInt24
- Updated audio buffer dtype from int16 to int32 (PyAudio compatible)
- Updated scaling factor from 32767 (16-bit) to 8388607 (24-bit: 2^23-1)
- Increased dynamic range from ~96dB to ~144dB
- Reduced quantization noise, professional music production standard
- Applied to all audio output paths: normal, silence, and exception handlers

24-bit provides ~48dB more dynamic range than 16-bit, essential for
subtle pad sounds and low-level signal clarity.

commit.message:
Prevent bfcache in Safari via BroadcastChannel

Chrome's unload listener doesn't work in Safari. A BroadcastChannel
with a registered message listener disqualifies the page from Safari's
bfcache, preventing orphaned AudioContext audio bleed across sessions.

commit.message:
feat(slides): add write support — create, shapes, tables, charts, delete

Add 7 new Slides subcommands for full write support via the Google
Slides batchUpdate API:

- create: create a new presentation
- add-slide: add slides with configurable layout
- add-shape: add shapes (rectangle, ellipse, text box, etc.) with
  optional text, fill color, font size, and bold styling
- add-table: add tables with optional data population and header color
- add-chart: embed Google Sheets charts (linked or static)
- delete: remove slides or page elements
- batch-update: raw batchUpdate passthrough for advanced use

Shapes support hex color fills with auto-white text on dark backgrounds.
Object IDs are auto-generated (uuid v4) or user-specified via --object-id.
